type_definitions.name: ReviewApprovalVerdict
type_definitions.description: Domain enum expressing the outcome of the approval/bypass check for the entire track's review cycle. Three variants: Approved (all scopes are NotRequired(*) — no scope requires review; check-approved exits [OK]; hint fires in sotp review results only when Approved AND review.json exists as a separate check by the results command); ApprovedWithBypass { not_started_count: usize } (all Required scopes are Required(NotStarted) AND review.json is absent — PR-based workflow bypass, no local review recorded; check-approved emits [WARN] + SUCCESS; not_started_count carries count of Required(NotStarted) scopes for the WARN message; hint does NOT fire); Blocked { required_scopes: Vec<ScopeName> } (one or more scopes still require review and the bypass condition does not apply; check-approved emits [BLOCKED] + EXIT_FAILURE). Introduced to lift the approval/bypass judgment from CLI layer (apps/cli/src/commands/review/mod.rs run_check_approved) into domain/usecase (IN-05, AC-09). The three-variant enum-first design (per 04-coding-principles.md Enum-first) eliminates the previous boolean-flag + required-scope struct anti-pattern: Approved and ApprovedWithBypass are structurally distinct (Bypass carries not_started_count for WARN messaging), and Blocked carries the required scopes so the CLI can render a human-readable list without re-querying states. No serde derives in domain layer (per CN-04 hexagonal architecture).

type_definitions.name: ScopeRound
type_definitions.description: Plain data struct holding the persisted state of one review round for a single scope. Introduced in T005 to carry the round-history payload that sotp review results reads when rendering state lines with latest-round suffix and expanding findings/history under --limit N. Fields: round_type: RoundType (fast | final), verdict: Verdict (ZeroFindings | FindingsRemain), findings: Vec<ReviewerFinding>, hash: ReviewHash, at: String (ISO 8601 timestamp). All four field types (RoundType, Verdict, ReviewerFinding, ReviewHash) are pre-existing domain types defined in libs/domain/src/review_v2/types.rs. hash is ReviewHash (Computed(ReviewHashValue) | Empty) rather than the narrower ReviewHashValue so that empty-hash rounds (stored as "" in review.json) can be represented without data loss — parallel with how read_latest_finals already handles empty-hash rounds. No serde derives in domain; infrastructure layer handles deserialization from review.json. If ReviewReader does not yet expose read_all_rounds, the method is added in the T005 commit: fn read_all_rounds(&self, scope: &ScopeName) -> Result<Vec<ScopeRound>, ReviewReaderError>.

type_definitions.name: ReviewReader
type_definitions.description: Secondary port (driven by infrastructure) for reading review.json v2 data. Pre-existing domain port — declared here as reference to complete the contract-map picture for FsReviewStore's implements edges. Two methods: read_latest_finals returns a HashMap of scope names to the latest final (Verdict, ReviewHash) pair; read_all_rounds (added in this track's T001) returns all rounds for a single scope in storage order (oldest first), returning an empty Vec when the scope has no rounds or review.json does not exist. Both methods return ReviewReaderError on I/O or codec failure.

type_definitions.name: ReviewWriter
type_definitions.description: Secondary port (driven by infrastructure) for writing to review.json v2. Pre-existing domain port — declared here as reference to complete the contract-map picture for FsReviewStore's implements edges. Four methods: write_verdict appends a final verdict with findings and hash to a scope's round history; write_fast_verdict appends a fast verdict; init creates a new review.json (track initialization); reset archives the existing review.json and creates a new one (review restart, does NOT clear .commit_hash). All methods return ReviewWriterError on I/O or codec failure. Implementations should use fs4::lock_exclusive for concurrent write safety.

type_definitions.name: ReviewExistsPort
type_definitions.description: Secondary port (driven by infrastructure) for querying whether review.json is present on disk for a given track. Abstracts the filesystem existence check from the usecase layer so the CLI composition root — not the ReviewCycle usecase — performs the I/O and converts the result to a bool for evaluate_approval. Placed in domain per hexagonal convention (secondary ports that abstract persistence I/O live in domain or usecase). The single method review_json_exists returns Result<bool, ReviewReaderError>: Ok(true) when the file exists and is accessible, Ok(false) when the file is absent (not found), Err(...) when an I/O error prevents determining existence (e.g., permission denied on a parent directory). The CLI composition root calls review_json_exists() and propagates the Err or maps it to a CLI-level error before passing the bool to evaluate_approval; this preserves the evaluate_approval signature (bool parameter) while ensuring I/O failures are not silently treated as absence. FsReviewStore in infrastructure implements this port alongside ReviewReader and ReviewWriter.
